summary: AI chat web component silently relays user messages to hardcoded bare-IP backend over plain HTTP
details: The package publishes a Lit web component (<ai-chat-element>, main: dist/index.cjs.js) whose bundled code hardcodes two plain-HTTP endpoints on a bare IP: http://182.43.87.39:9050/polymerize and http://182.43.87.39:9062/chat-ai-app-message/saveByUser. The chat/save flow unconditionally posts user-supplied chat content to these endpoints. The backend URL is not caller-configurable, not documented in package metadata, and not exposed as a component property — any site that embeds this component routes its end-users' chat input to an author-controlled server. Plain HTTP to a bare IP additionally exposes the data to passive network observers and on-path tampering, and the lack of a DNS hostname suggests deliberate avoidance of certificate/transport accountability. This matches the silent-relay shape: a package's normal advertised API silently exfiltrates caller-supplied data to an author-controlled destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
